transcript.clean_text: first stop of the twenty20 men's world tour begins with stage one of the Santos Tour Down Under 150 kilometers broken down into five 30 kilometre circuits starting and finishing in turn under the ominously named breakneck Hill features on the circuit with king of the mountains points awarded on the second and fourth time round but only 400 metres long it's unlikely to unsettle the sprinters too much and we should see the likes of Caleb Ewan Elia Viviani and Sam Bennett going head-to-head for the first ochre jersey of the race defending our jersey from 2019 Daryl Impey set an early throw down to his GC competitors swapping the first intermediate sprint of the day after 15 kilometres and it wasn't long after that we've had our first breakaway of the race a quartet of riders featuring Joey Roscoe of CCC Michael Storer of Sun Webb Dylan's Sunderland event et and jarred dresner's riding for the All Australian uni s a team who take the first king of the mountains points at kilometer 46 and he wasn't done there 30 kilometres later he'd go on to win the second and final intermediate sprint of the day for two laps remaining the time gap was a minute and 30 seconds as the peloton crossed the finish line coffered is leading the way to Kenan quickstep and a lot o su dal in close attendance ahead ross confer tack for 55 kilometers still to go it's a real workhorse of the peloton and a WS National Time Trial champion but today he was eyeing the Polka Dot Jersey he muscled his way to the line at kilometer 106 to take the bonuses with he and risen as level on five points apiece we'd have to wait until the finish to that countback decide who gets to wear the jersey tomorrow the American didn't seem too keen on taking on a fresh peloton on the first proper day of racing this year and he was quickly absorbed just before we came around for the fifth and final lap the body language of the pack changing in an instant Votto sudah motivated and ready to execute their lead-out train in support of Caleb Ewan who looked mightily impressive at the Schwab a classic a couple of days ago positioning very important on the running especially with the speeds already hi is where the sprinters setting up for the win we also had GC riders in the mix Richie Porte well protected by world champion mass Pettersen is he in standard of any US hit the front to drive the pace perhaps Chris lawless could deliver a win for the British squad in the finale though it was to kerning quickstep and Michel Merc who the Danish champion setting up the sprint for new signing Sam Bennett the Irishman was dropped off just under 200 meters to go there he goes on the right-hand side of your picture Jasper's phillotson of UAE on the left it was a close run thing but Bennett Jia edged it instant payback for his new team - curling quickstep his talent in the sprint is no secret whatsoever and with the personnel it is disposal for the Belgian super team this could be a year to remember for the Irish national champion you can see from the aerial shot that Phillips them got the better acceleration but Bennett's power held all the way to the line as the Belgian faded towards the end very Baskar on sprint duty for Bennett's former team Bora hands Dre will hopefully be encouraged by a third-place finish today they can tell it's a hard and fast sprint when the riders don't even dare to take their hands off the bars in celebration it's a big win for Bennett here is your top 10 and Viviani rifle and you are not quite able to compete today although there will be plenty more chances for them to shine this week so two for Christopher Howell listen his EF team were up there at the finish and samwell's foot of uni se definitely want to watch later this week ten seconds in time bonuses at the line means Bennett takes the overall lead - and your standings after stage one looked like this Daryl Impey slotting into fourth at seven seconds on the same time as jarred prisoners who would finish ahead of Rosco at the finish to take that Polka Dot Jersey tomorrow her tale of two circuits laps around the start town of Woodside followed by a foray into the adelaide hills and a further three laps of the familiar loop between miler and sterling with over 2300 meters of elevation in 138 kilometers the sprinters will really have to graph for the win tomorrow especially with a cruel uphill drag to the line that could suit someone more like Daryl Impey J McCarthy or even Tiago Alesi whose talents have suited this type of finish in the past don't forget to subscribe to GCN racing to make sure you don't miss an intriguing second stage
